title: Guides
subtitle: Learn to set up and maintain your aquarium
cyclingDesc: Cycling is the most important process before adding fish to the aquarium. It takes 4 to 6 weeks for beneficial bacteria to establish.
type.community.title: Freshwater Community
type.community.desc: Aquarium with diverse peaceful species living together.
type.community.fish: Neon Tetra, Corydoras, Betta (alone), Rasbora, Guppy, Platy
type.planted.title: Planted Aquarium
type.planted.desc: Focus on aquatic plants with lighting and CO2.
type.planted.fish: Neon Tetra, Rasbora, Otocinclus, Corydoras, Amano Shrimp
type.cichlid.title: African Cichlids
type.cichlid.desc: Aquarium with cichlids from Lake Malawi or Tanganyika.
type.marine.title: Marine / Reef Aquarium
type.marine.desc: Saltwater aquarium with fish, corals and invertebrates.
type.marine.fish: Amphiprion, Chromis, Gramma loreto, Gobiodon, Nemateleotris
type.community.steps: Choose an aquarium of at least 60 liters
Add inert substrate (gravel or sand) 3-5cm deep
Install the filter (minimum 5x the volume per hour) and heater
Decorate with driftwood and rocks (create hiding spots)
Fill with dechlorinated water and turn on equipment
Start with hardy fish like Danios or Corydoras
Add new species gradually (2-3 per week)
type.planted.steps: Choose an aquarium with good surface area (low and wide)
Place fertile substrate at the base covered by fine gravel
Install adequate lighting (0.5-1W per liter or LED equivalent)
Set up CO2 system (pressurized or DIY)
Plant background plants first, then midground and foreground
Start with reduced lighting (6h/day) and increase gradually
Wait for cycling and plants to establish (3-4 weeks)
Add peaceful small fish that don't damage plants
type.cichlid.steps: Aquarium of at least 200 liters (cichlids are territorial)
Aragonite sand or limestone gravel substrate (raises pH)
Build many rocks creating caves and territories
Powerful filtration (8-10x the volume per hour)
Do not use plants (most will be destroyed)
Add several fish at once to dilute aggression
Maintain controlled overstocking to reduce territories
type.marine.steps: Aquarium of at least 150 liters with sump
Install skimmer, circulation pump and marine LED lighting
Prepare water with synthetic salt made for marine aquariums
Wait for cycling with live rock (4-8 weeks)
Add soft corals first, then LPS and finally SPS
cycling.step1.title: Setup
cycling.step1.desc: Set up the aquarium with substrate, decoration, filter and heater. Fill with dechlorinated water and turn everything on.
cycling.step2.title: Ammonia source
cycling.step2.desc: Add an ammonia source: fish food, dead shrimp or pure ammonia (2-4 ppm).
cycling.step3.title: Ammonia spike
cycling.step3.desc: Ammonia rises. Nitrosomonas bacteria begin to colonize the filter. Test ammonia every 2 days.
cycling.step4.title: Nitrite appears
cycling.step4.desc: Ammonia starts dropping and nitrite rises. Nitrospira bacteria are establishing.
cycling.step5.title: Nitrite drops
cycling.step5.desc: Nitrite starts dropping and nitrate appears. The cycle is almost complete.
cycling.step6.title: Cycle complete
cycling.step6.desc: Ammonia 0, Nitrite 0, Nitrate present. Do a 50% PWC and add the first fish!
